books.title: Atomic Habits
books.author: James Clear
books.description: An easy and proven way to build good habits and break bad ones, focusing on tiny changes that yield remarkable results.

books.title: The Power of Habit
books.author: Charles Duhigg
books.description: Explores the thrilling edge of scientific discoveries that explain why habits exist and how they can be changed.

books.title: Deep Work
books.author: Cal Newport
books.description: Rules for focused success in a distracted world, teaching how to cultivate a deep work ethic.

books.title: Thinking, Fast and Slow
books.author: Daniel Kahneman
books.description: A groundbreaking tour of the mind explaining the two systems that drive the way we think.

books.title: Mindset: The New Psychology of Success
books.author: Carol S. Dweck
books.description: Shows how success in school, work, sports, the arts, and almost every area of human endeavor can be dramatically influenced by how we think about our talents and abilities.

books.title: Essentialism: The Disciplined Pursuit of Less
books.author: Greg McKeown
books.description: A systematic discipline for discerning what is absolutely essential, then eliminating everything that is not.

books.title: Tiny Habits
books.author: BJ Fogg
books.description: The small changes that change everything, introducing the Fogg Behavior Model to form habits quickly and easily.

books.title: Make Time
books.author: Jake Knapp, John Zeratsky
books.description: How to focus on what matters every day, offering a customizable menu of bite-size tips and strategies.

books.title: Grit: The Power of Passion and Perseverance
books.author: Angela Duckworth
books.description: Shows anyone striving to succeed that the secret to outstanding achievement is not talent, but a special blend of passion and persistence.

books.title: Drive: The Surprising Truth About What Motivates Us
books.author: Daniel H. Pink
books.description: Examines the three elements of true motivation—autonomy, mastery, and purpose—and offers smart and surprising techniques for putting these into action.

books.title: The 7 Habits of Highly Effective People
books.author: Stephen R. Covey
books.description: Presents a holistic, integrated, principle-centered approach for solving personal and professional problems.

books.title: Eat That Frog!
books.author: Brian Tracy
books.description: 21 Great Ways to Stop Procrastinating and Get More Done in Less Time.

books.title: Outliers: The Story of Success
books.author: Malcolm Gladwell
books.description: An examination of the factors that contribute to high levels of success, arguing that we pay too much attention to what successful people are like, and too little attention to where they are from.

books.title: How to Win Friends and Influence People
books.author: Dale Carnegie
books.description: A timeless bestseller packed with rock-solid advice that has carried thousands of now famous people up the ladder of success in their business and personal lives.

books.title: Quiet: The Power of Introverts in a World That Can't Stop Talking
books.author: Susan Cain
books.description: Explores the rise of the Extrovert Ideal throughout the 20th century and explores how deeply it has come to permeate our culture, dramatically undervaluing introverts.

books.title: The 4-Hour Workweek
books.author: Timothy Ferriss
books.description: A step-by-step guide to luxury lifestyle design, teaching how to escape the 9-5, live anywhere, and join the new rich using modern tools and outsourcing.

books.title: Man's Search for Meaning
books.author: Viktor E. Frankl
books.description: Psychiatrist Viktor Frankl's memoir has riveted generations of readers with its descriptions of life in Nazi death camps and its profound lessons for spiritual survival and finding purpose.
